data.content:
## Diagnosis

This is a **tiny project** (1 source file, ~45 LOC) drowning in **analyzer overhead**. Here's where build time is going:

### Analyzer Stack (5 packages!)

| Source | Analyzer Package |
|--------|-----------------|
| `Directory.Build.props` | `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers` (global) |
| `Directory.Build.props` | `StyleCop.Analyzers` (global) |
| `.csproj` | `Roslynator.Analyzers` |
| `.csproj` | `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp` |
| `.csproj` | `Meziantou.Analyzer` |

### Compounding Factors in `Directory.Build.props`

- **`EnforceCodeStyleInBuild=true`** — runs all IDE code-style rules as build-time analyzers
- **`AnalysisLevel=latest-recommended`** — maximizes the built-in analyzer set
- **`GenerateDocumentationFile=true`** — XML doc generation adds an extra pass
- **`TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`** — forces all analyzers to fully complete (can't skip early)

---

## Recommended Fixes (by impact)

### 1. Consolidate overlapping analyzers
`Roslynator`, `SonarAnalyzer`, `Meziantou`, `NetAnalyzers`, and `StyleCop` have massive rule overlap. Pick **one or two**, not five. Recommendation: keep `NetAnalyzers` (built-in) + one of `Meziantou` or `Roslynator`.

### 2. Move heavy analysis out of `build` and into CI/IDE only
```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>false</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
```
Run `dotnet format --verify-no-changes` in CI instead — same enforcement, zero inner-loop cost.

### 3. Restrict analyzers to `CI` builds only
For remaining third-party analyzers, condition them:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="SonarAnalyzer.CSharp" Version="9.16.0.82469"
                  Condition="'$(ContinuousIntegrationBuild)' == 'true'" />
```

### 4. Drop `GenerateDocumentationFile` unless publishing a library
This project is a WebApi (not a NuGet package consumed by others). XML docs add overhead with no consumer benefit.

---

### TL;DR
The project compiles in milliseconds but runs **5 overlapping Roslyn analyzer packages** on every build. Reduce to 1–2 analyzers and defer style enforcement to CI.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. This is a tiny project (1 source file, ~45 lines of code) that is loaded with analyzers. Let me diagnose the build time issues:

1. **Directory.Build.props** applies globally:
   - `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true` - runs code style analysis during build
   - `AnalysisLevel>latest-recommended` - enables all recommended analyzers
   - `GenerateDocumentationFile>true` - generates XML docs
   - `ProduceReferenceAssembly>true` - generates reference assemblies
   - Two global analyzer packages: `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers` and `StyleCop.Analyzers`
   
2. **Contoso.WebApi.csproj** piles on three more analyzers: `Roslynator.Analyzers`, `SonarAnalyzer.CSharp`, and `Meziantou.Analyzer`. So I'm looking at five analyzer packages total running on a single-file project with code style enforcement enabled—this is a textbook case of analyzer bloat where the actual compilation is instant but the analyzers themselves are eating up all the build time.
